creator: bartsch@dssgmbh.de
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Am 13.09.2012 22:49, schrieb Bryan Drewery:
> I was able to build devel/libftdi and comms/lirc in 8.3 i386 jail
> with poudriere:
> 
> ====>> 45 packages built, 0 failures, 0 ignored, 0 skipped ====>>
> Built ports: devel/ccache devel/pkgconf devel/libtool x11/xtrans 
> x11/xproto x11-fonts/xf86bigfontproto security/p11-kit
> x11/xcmiscproto devel/autoconf-wrapper x11/libXau x11/libXdmcp
> devel/xorg-macros devel/m4 x11/xextproto devel/automake-wrapper
> multimedia/v4l_compat x11/inputproto x11/bigreqsproto x11/kbproto
> converters/libiconv lang/perl5.14 devel/gettext math/gmp
> security/libgpg-error devel/gmake devel/p5-Locale-gettext
> devel/libcheck misc/help2man comms/libirman devel/libpthread-stubs
> devel/autoconf devel/automake textproc/libxml2 devel/libftdi
> textproc/odt2txt security/nettle security/gnutls lang/python27
> x11/xcb-proto textproc/iksemel security/libgcrypt textproc/libxslt
> x11/libxcb x11/libX11 comms/lirc
> 
> 
> Can you please make sure your poudriere ports tree is up-to-date.
> Run poudriere ports -u.
> 
> Bryan

The last update of devel/libftdi on 09/11 (!) seems to have solved
this issue. Now I'm able to build this port flawlessly, even with
poudriere. Thanks for your time.

- -- 
Regards
Alfred Bartsch
Data-Service GmbH
